fear: The silence behind him—the knowledge that the fortifications are empty and that if the British come, there is no one to fall back on.
name: Corporal Nathaniel Hutchins
role: Rear Guard Sentry, Brooklyn Heights Parapet
voice: Formal beyond his years when addressing officers; speaks in complete sentences as if reciting a drill manual, which is how he manages fear.
knowledge: The exact fuel inventory at his specific fire station: fewer than a dozen usable pine logs remain.
That a British picket shouted a challenge toward the parapet approximately forty minutes ago; the challenge was not answered and nothing happened, but the proximity was closer than before.
The countersign for tonight, which he will require from any runner approaching his position in the dark.
publicFace: A Connecticut farm boy of nineteen, assigned to the rear guard because his regiment drew the short rotation; performs his post with mechanical discipline that masks genuine terror.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Reports his status accurately and asks for orders.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not leave his post without a direct order from a commissioned officer, regardless of what he hears behind him.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Admits the unauthorized fuel rationing and states his reasoning without excuse.

privateConstraint: Hutchins is responsible for the left parapet campfire station, where the fuel supply is running lowest; he has been rationing pine logs without authorization because he is afraid of running out before the order to embark arrives.
